risk_if_not_implemented: Without Data Subject Attribute Associations, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Cybersecurity & Data Protection Attributes
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to bind cybersecurity and data protection attributes to information as it is stored, transmitted and processed.
compensating_control_1.justification: Cybersecurity & Data Protection Attributes (DCH-05) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Data Subject Attribute Associations (DCH-05.6) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Data & Asset Classification
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure data and assets are categorized in accordance with applicable statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements.
compensating_control_2.justification: Data & Asset Classification (DCH-02) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Data Subject Attribute Associations (DCH-05.6) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
